Lesotho generally experiences very minimal conflict, with the ACLED baseline average of 'strategic developments' essentially at 0 over the 360 days leading up to October 2025. To cross the threshold required by the question (10-fold increase over the baseline of 1), Lesotho would need to experience 11 or more 'strategic developments' (such as large-scale arrests, military movements, or political violence) within the referenced 30-day windows in October, November, or December 2025/January 2026. A thorough search of news and crisis databases covering late 2025 and early 2026 shows no evidence of such events occurring within Lesotho. In fact, South African Development Community (SADC) leaders specifically removed Lesotho from their crisis agenda around early 2026 citing "positive progress in reforms and stability." Aside from isolated items like the arrest of two soldiers selling arms or Lesotho's PM holding meetings in South Africa, there was no major unrest or wave of ACLED-codable non-violent tactical/strategic maneuvers. Therefore, it is virtually certain that the count did not reach 11+ in any of the stipulated 30-day periods. With all resolution dates now in the past, I assign a 2% baseline probability due to the theoretical minor risk of sudden retroactive ACLED data dumps.

The 360-day average of ~128 protests/month was sustained by the Hostages Families Forum organizing protests across 50-80 nationwide locations every week. In October 2025, a hostage and ceasefire deal was finalized (Trump's deadline was Oct 5, Israeli cabinet approved it around Oct 10). After the deal, widespread grassroots hostage protests largely ended, with the Families Forum actively winding down and closing by Dec 31. To exceed the 128 mark in a 30-day period, an average of ~32 separate protest events per week is required. 
1. The 30 days before Nov 2 (Oct 3 - Nov 2) include 1 week of peak pre-deal protests but 3 weeks of post-deal lull, making it highly probable the total falls short of the 128 threshold (which requires 4 active weekends).
2. The 30 days before Nov 25 (Oct 26 - Nov 25) sit firmly in the post-deal lull, with very few hostage protests and before anti-government protests fully resumed, likely resulting in a very low event count.
3. The 30 days before Jan 24 (Dec 25 - Jan 24) cover the resurgence of weekly anti-government protests following Netanyahu's December 2025 pardon request. However, purely political rallies tend to concentrate thousands of people into a few major cities rather than dozens of local junctions, making it doubtful they will exceed the wartime geographical spread measured by ACLED. 
Uncertainty increases for more distant dates as new issues (like the Lebanon anti-war movement) could shift dynamics, naturally regressing predictions closer to 0.5.

4) Period 3 (30 days before 2026-01-24): This specific window (Dec 25 - Jan 23) narrowly misses the severe Dec 18-20 riots over the assassination of a youth leader. Human rights monitoring noted that Jan 2026 saw only 4 political deaths (up from 1 in Dec 2025). The intense police crackdown ahead of the February national elections seems to have kept political fatalities low, meaning this period is likely closer to or below the baseline. Following instructions, the probabilities converge towards 0.50 for the most distant horizon.
